selection_process: Selection under IBPS CRP SPL-XVI 2026 follows a three-stage process: Online Preliminary Examination
and a Common Interview. Only candidates who clear each stage progress to the next
and the final merit list is based on Main Examination and Interview scores combined.

Online Preliminary Examination – Tentatively in August 2026; candidates must clear individual cut-offs in each of four tests. Preliminary Result – Declared in September/October 2026. Online Main Examination – Tentatively in November 2026; scores from this stage alone are used for interview shortlisting and final merit. Main Exam Result – Declared in November 2026. Common Interview – Conducted by Participating Banks and coordinated by Nodal Banks in November/December 2026; total 100 marks with minimum qualifying marks of 40% (35% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD). Provisional Allotment – Expected in January 2027
based on merit-cum-preference with an 80:20 weightage between Main Exam and Interview.
The Preliminary Exam for IT Officer
and Marketing Officer consists of 100 questions for 125 marks across English Language
to be completed in 80 minutes. The Main Exam for the same posts carries 150 objective questions for 200 marks plus a Descriptive Paper of 25 marks

totaling 225 marks in 155 minutes. Law Officer and Rajbhasha Adhikari have a similar structure but include General Awareness with Special Reference to Banking Industry in place of Quantitative Aptitude.
There is a penalty of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in objective tests; no penalty applies for unattempted questions.

content_sections.text: Candidates who want to verify complete details before applying should download the official IBPS CRP SPL-XVI notification PDF, which contains eligibility criteria, exam pattern, vacancy annexures, examination centres, and certificate formats. The detailed notification titled "Detailed-Notification-CRP-SPL-XVI" is dated 01.07.2026 and is available for download on the official IBPS website. Download IBPS SO Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Here Direct Link to Apply Online for IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Recruitment 2026

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP SPL-XVI 2026 - Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives a snapshot of the IBPS Specialist Officer recruitment 2026 so candidates can quickly check the key details before reading the full notification breakdown. The table below covers the organization name, total vacancies, application deadline, and mode of application.

rows.ibps_crp_spl_xvi_specialist_officers: IT Officer, Agricultural Field Officer, Rajbhasha Adhikari, Law Officer, HR/Personnel Officer, Marketing Officer (Scale I)

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates preparing for IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 need to track the complete exam calendar, from registration to provisional allotment. All dates listed below are tentative and sourced directly from the official notification dated 01.07.2026.

rows.tentative_schedule: 2 days after closing of registration (dates to be notified)

rows.activity: Download of Call Letter – Preliminary Exam
Download of Call Letter – Main Exam

content_sections.heading: IBPS Specialist Officer Vacancy Details 2026 (Post-wise Breakdown)
content_sections.text: The IBPS CRP SPL-XVI vacancy 2026 covers six Specialist Officer posts distributed across 11 participating banks, with IT Officer carrying the highest number of openings. These vacancies are indicative and based on the actual requirement reported by each bank for the financial year 2027-28. Participating banks include Bank of Baroda, Bank of India, Bank of Maharashtra, Canara Bank, Central Bank of India, Indian Bank, Indian Overseas Bank, Punjab National Bank, Punjab & Sind Bank, UCO Bank, and Union Bank of India.

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 depends on the specific post applied for, since each role has a distinct educational qualification requirement. Age limits, however, remain uniform across all six posts, with relaxations available for reserved categories. The minimum age required is 20 years and the maximum age is 30 years , meaning candidates must be born not earlier than 02.07.1996 and not later than 01.07.2006. For posts other than IT Officer, candidates must also have studied Computer/Information Technology as a subject in High School, College, or Institute, or hold a certificate/diploma/degree in computer operations.

rows.qualification_required: 4-year Engineering /Technology degree in Computer Science/IT/Electronics or related field, OR Postgraduate degree in relevant field, OR Graduate with DOEACC 'B' level
4-year degree in Agriculture/Horticulture/Animal Husbandry/Veterinary Science/Dairy Science/Fishery Science or allied subjects
Postgraduate degree in Hindi with English as a subject at graduation, OR PG in Sanskrit with English and Hindi as subjects
Bachelor's Degree in Law ( LL.B ) and enrolled as an advocate with Bar Council
Graduate and 2-year full-time PG degree/ diploma in Personnel Management/HR/Industrial Relations/Social Work/Labour Law
Graduate and 2-year full-time MBA/MMS/PGDBA/PGDM with specialization in Marketing

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 varies by category and must be paid online during the registration window. Bank transaction charges for online payment are borne separately by the candidate. Payment can be made using Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, Mobile Wallets, or UPI.

content_sections.heading: IBPS Specialist Officer Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection under IBPS CRP SPL-XVI 2026 follows a three-stage process: Online Preliminary Examination, Online Main Examination, and a Common Interview. Only candidates who clear each stage progress to the next, and the final merit list is based on Main Examination and Interview scores combined. Online Preliminary Examination – Tentatively in August 2026; candidates must clear individual cut-offs in each of four tests. Preliminary Result – Declared in September/October 2026. Online Main Examination – Tentatively in November 2026; scores from this stage alone are used for interview shortlisting and final merit. Main Exam Result – Declared in November 2026. Common Interview – Conducted by Participating Banks and coordinated by Nodal Banks in November/December 2026; total 100 marks with minimum qualifying marks of 40% (35% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD). Provisional Allotment – Expected in January 2027, based on merit-cum-preference with an 80:20 weightage between Main Exam and Interview. The Preliminary Exam for IT Officer, Agricultural Field Officer, HR/Personnel Officer, and Marketing Officer consists of 100 questions for 125 marks across English Language, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and Professional Knowledge, to be completed in 80 minutes. The Main Exam for the same posts carries 150 objective questions for 200 marks plus a Descriptive Paper of 25 marks, totaling 225 marks in 155 minutes. Law Officer and Rajbhasha Adhikari have a similar structure but include General Awareness with Special Reference to Banking Industry in place of Quantitative Aptitude. There is a penalty of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in objective tests; no penalty applies for unattempted questions.

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Apply Online 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply for IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 only through the online mode on the official IBPS website; no other application method is accepted. The steps below outline the complete registration and payment process. Visit the official website www.ibps.in and click on the "CRP SPL" link on the homepage. Select "Click Here to Apply Online for CRP-Specialist Officers (CRP SPL-XVI)" to open the application form. Click "Click Here for New Registration" and enter basic details to generate a Provisional Registration Number and Password. Log in again using the provisional credentials to complete the application form. Upload photograph, signature, left thumb impression, handwritten declaration, and SSC/SSLC/10th standard certificate as per specifications. Capture and upload a live photograph via webcam or mobile phone. Verify all details carefully, since no changes are allowed after clicking "Complete Registration" (except during the 2-day Edit Window). Pay the applicable application fee online via Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI. Download and print the system-generated application form and e-receipt for future reference. Direct Link to Apply Online for IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Recruitment 2026

content_sections.heading: IBPS Specialist Officer Salary and Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: Selected candidates under IBPS CRP SPL-XVI will be appointed as Specialist Officers, Scale I, with pay determined by the standard officer pay scale applicable across Participating Banks. Officials are also eligible for allowances and perquisites as per each bank's applicable rules. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP SPL-XVI Document Requirements
content_sections.text: Shortlisted candidates must produce original documents along with self-attested photocopies at the time of interview, failing which their candidature can be cancelled. Required documents include the interview call letter, system-generated application printout, proof of date of birth, photo identity proof, educational mark sheets/certificates, and category certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) as applicable, all in prescribed Government of India formats. OBC candidates must submit a Non-Creamy Layer certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 , while EWS candidates need an Income & Asset Certificate valid for the year 2026-27 for financial year 2025-26.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for IBPS CRP SPL-XVI
content_sections.text: Many candidates lose their chance at IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 due to avoidable errors during the application process. Being aware of these common mistakes in advance can help ensure a smooth, error-free submission. Applying for multiple posts: A candidate can apply for only one post; multiple applications are summarily rejected. Uploading incorrect photo/signature size: Photos must be 4.5cm × 3.5cm (20-50 KB), and signatures 10-20 KB; oversized or blurred files cause application rejection. Writing the declaration in capital letters: The handwritten declaration must be in the candidate's own handwriting, in English, and NOT in capital letters. Ignoring the Edit Window: Since most fields like Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, and Nationality cannot be edited after submission, double-check all entries before final submission. Missing the fee payment deadline: Applying without completing fee payment within 01.07.2026 to 21.07.2026 renders the form incomplete and invalid. Not retaining photograph copies: Candidates should keep at least 8 copies of the uploaded photograph, as these are required at multiple stages including exam day.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for IBPS CRP SPL-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 is 21 July 2026, with registration having opened on 01 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in IBPS CRP SPL-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: There are 745 indicative vacancies across six Specialist Officer posts, including IT Officer, Agricultural Field Officer, Law Officer, Rajbhasha Adhikari, and HR/Personnel Officer.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for IBPS Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 20 and 30 years of age as on 01.07.2026, with age relaxation available for SC/ST, OBC, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for IBPS CRP SPL-XVI?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹175 for SC/ST/PwBD candidates and ₹850 for all other candidates, payable online only.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for IBPS Specialist Officer posts?
faq.answer: Selection involves an Online Preliminary Examination, an Online Main Examination, and a Common Interview, with the final merit based on Main Exam and Interview scores in an 80:20 ratio.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for IBPS Specialist Officer Scale I?
faq.answer: The basic pay scale is ₹48,480-2000/7-62,480-2340/2-67,160-2680/7-85,920, along with allowances and perquisites as per the concerned Participating Bank's rules.

faq.question: Q7. Can a candidate apply for more than one post in CRP SPL-XVI?
faq.answer: No, candidates can apply for only one post; multiple applications will be summarily rejected and only the latest completed application is retained.

faq.question: Q8. When will the Online Preliminary Examination be conducted?
faq.answer: The Online Preliminary Examination is tentatively scheduled for August 2026, with results expected in September/October 2026.

faq.question: Q9. What is the qualifying mark for the interview round?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifying mark in the interview is 40% for general candidates and 35% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates, out of a total of 100 marks.

faq.question: Q10. Where can candidates check the official IBPS CRP SPL-XVI notification?
faq.answer: The complete notification, along with the apply online link, is available on the official IBPS website at www.ibps.in.
